Defendant Saw Entertainment Ltd, A California Corporation'S Motion For Prevailing Party Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Aug-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, R. Michael Flynn of Flynn Law Office (510-893-3226) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Shane M. Cahill of Long & Levit LLP (415-438-4594) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendant Saw Entertainment Ltd's Motion For Prevailing Party Attorneys Fees And Costs is STAYED. This case is on appeal, so consideration of the motion is STAYED. (CCP 916; Western Steel & Ship Repair, Inc. (1986) 176 Cal.App.3d 1108, 1116 ("inherent power of courts to control their processes and orders").) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR 176, email: geales@sftc.org. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-13-2016 Continued To Dec-28-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Between Plaintiff Samuel Rodriguez And Defendant (C.C.P 664.6)
Off Calendar Apr-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Michael Flynn of Flynn Law Office telephone number 510-893-3226 for Plaintiff Samuel Rodriguez.
Shane Cahill of Long & Levit LLP telephone number 415-438-4594 for Defendant Saw Entertainment LTD.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAW ENTERTAINMENT LTD's Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement is granted. The settlement agreement is clear and unambiguous that it applies to both the personal injury and wage and hour lawsuits. The settlement agreement meets all the requirements for enforcement under CCP 664.6. All of Mr. Rodriguez's arguments opposing the motion lack merit. There was no trickery or deception by SAW or its counsel. Nor was there any violation of CRPC 2-100. Whether or not his personal injury attorneys had the authority to settle the wage and hour case is immaterial since Mr. Rodriguez signed the settlement agreement and he had the authority to dismiss his own claims. Meeting of the mind is determined from the agreement itself, not from Mr. Roriguez's or his attorney's own thoughts that were never communicated to SAW or its counsel prior to execution of the agreement. Unilateral mistake is not a basis to avoid the agreement since failure to read a contract before it is signed constitutes neglect of a legal duty. There is nothing procedurally or substantively unconscionable about the settlement agreement. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-16-2015 Continued To Jun-29-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-14-2015 Continued To Dec-30-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Mar-25-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. R. MICHAEL FLYNN of Flynn Law Office, Tel No. (510) 893-3226 appearing for the Plaintiffs;
Atty. SHANE M. CAHILL of Long & Levit LLP, Tel No. (415) 438-4594 appearing for Defendant Saw Entertainment LTD dba Hustler Club.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAW ENTERTAINMENT LTD DBA HUSTLER CLUB'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action is granted. Under CCP Sec. 1281.2, a court may deny a motion to compel arbitration when a party to the arbitration agreement is also a party to a pending court action with a third party that arises out of the same transaction or series of related transactions and presents a possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact. Henry v. Alcove Inv., Inc., (1991) 233 Cal. App. 3d 94. Plaintiffs have not established that Defendant has waived his right to compel arbitration. Further Plaintiffs have not shown a possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact. The arbitration agreement explicitly includes any and all claims arising from the employment relationship. Plaintiff's claims arise under the employment relationship, therefore are within the scope of the arbitration agreement. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
